prompt: Find where the GIL (Global Interpreter Lock) is acquired and released during thread switching
notes: Category: single-file impl. Tests navigation to core threading/concurrency machinery

prompt: Find the implementation of list.append and how it handles dynamic array resizing
notes: Category: single-file impl. Tests navigation to a well-known built-in method

prompt: Find where Python dict lookup is implemented and how hash collision resolution works
notes: Category: single-file impl. Tests navigation to dict internals

prompt: Find where the bytecode compiler generates LOAD_FAST and STORE_FAST instructions for local variables
notes: Category: single-file impl. Tests navigation to the compiler/codegen layer

prompt: Find the CPython garbage collector's cycle detection algorithm
notes: Category: single-file impl. Tests navigation to the cyclic GC

prompt: Trace how Python resolves attribute lookup through the MRO (Method Resolution Order) for new-style classes
notes: Category: algorithm within subsystem. MRO traversal is a multi-step algorithm inside typeobject.c

prompt: Find the entry point where CPython parses command-line arguments and initializes sys.argv
notes: Category: entry point discovery. Tests ability to find interpreter startup logic

prompt: Trace how a SyntaxError during compilation gets from the parser to the point where it is raised as a Python exception
notes: Category: cross-file concept. Requires tracing error propagation across parser and runtime layers

prompt: Find where Python's iterator protocol (__iter__ and __next__) is dispatched in the abstract object layer
notes: Category: protocol/interface impl. Tests navigation to the abstract C API for iteration

prompt: Add a comment to PyObject_Init in the object implementation explaining why the initial reference count is set to 1
notes: Category: edit / 'add'. Requires finding PyObject_Init in Objects/object.c and inserting an explanatory comment

prompt: Change the error message raised when a non-callable object is called to include the word 'not callable' if it does not already
notes: Category: edit / 'change'. Requires finding the non-callable TypeError in Objects/typeobject.c or Objects/abstract.c

prompt: Fix the collect function in the cyclic garbage collector by adding a comment at the top of the function body stating which generation it starts collecting from
notes: Category: edit / 'fix'. Requires locating Python/gc.c collect entry point and inserting documentation

prompt: Update the comment above the GIL drop logic in the eval loop to note that it yields to other threads at safe points
notes: Category: edit / 'update'. Requires finding GIL drop in Python/ceval.c or Python/ceval_gil.c

prompt: Remove the most prominent TODO or FIXME comment from the symtable analysis code

prompt: Rename the local variable used to hold the lookup result in _PyDict_GetItem_KnownHash to make its purpose clearer — change it from a single-letter name to a descriptive one
notes: Category: edit / 'rename'. Requires navigating to Objects/dictobject.c and doing a local variable rename

prompt: Refactor the overallocation calculation in list_resize so the growth formula is assigned to a named variable before being used, instead of being computed inline
notes: Category: edit / 'refactor'. Requires finding Objects/listobject.c list_resize and extracting an expression into a named variable
